name: Calar Alto — circular desde Gérgal
region_intro: Touring cycling routes around Gérgal traverse a diverse landscape situated between the Sierra de los Filabres mountain range and the Tabernas Desert. The region features significant elevation changes, with winding roads through mountainous terrain and arid, "Wild West" scenery in the desert. Cyclists encounter varied gradients, from gentle slopes to challenging ascents, with options for both paved and unpaved surfaces. This area offers a wide range of terrain, including Mediterranean vegetation in the mountains and unique desert badlands. Best touring cycling routes around Gérgal The most popular touring cycling route is Calar Alto — circular desde Gérgal, a 36.1 miles (58.2 km) trail that takes 4 hours 25 minutes to complete, featuring a significant climb to the Calar Alto Observatory. Another top favourite among local touring cyclists is Gérgal, Olula de Castro and Tabernas Desert Loop, a difficult 40.6 miles (65.3 km) path that explores the unique arid landscapes of the Tabernas Desert. Local touring cyclists also love the Gérgal and Santa Fe de Mondú, a 36.8 miles (59.2 km) trail leading through local villages and surrounding terrain, often completed in about 4 hours 29 minutes. Touring cycling around Gérgal is defined by the mountainous Sierra de los Filabres, the arid Tabernas Desert, and varied elevation profiles. The network offers options for different ability levels, from moderate rides to challenging ascents. The routes in Gérgal are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.2 stars from over 20 reviews. More than 500 touring cyclists have used komoot to explore Gérgal's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many touring cycling routes are available around Gérgal?
A: Gérgal offers a wide selection of touring cycling routes, with over 50 different options to explore. These routes cater to various skill levels, from moderate rides to very challenging ascents.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on touring cycling routes in Gérgal?
A: The terrain around Gérgal is incredibly diverse, situated between the mountainous Sierra de los Filabres and the arid Tabernas Desert. You can expect significant elevation changes with winding roads through the mountains, featuring Mediterranean vegetation. In contrast, routes through the Tabernas Desert offer unique 'Wild West' scenery, badlands, and can include segments with loose gravel and unpaved surfaces. There are options for both paved and mixed-surface rides.

Q: What is the best time of year for touring cycling in Gérgal?
A: Gérgal enjoys over 3,000 hours of sunshine annually, making it suitable for cycling for much of the year. However, for touring cycling, spring (March to May) and autumn (September to November) are generally ideal. During these seasons, temperatures are milder, avoiding the intense heat of summer and the cooler, potentially wetter conditions of winter, especially at higher elevations in the Sierra de los Filabres.

Q: Are there many challenging touring cycling routes in Gérgal?
A: Yes, Gérgal is well-known for its challenging touring cycling routes. A significant majority of the routes, 46 out of 54, are classified as difficult. These often involve substantial climbs, such as the ascent to the Calar Alto Observatory, and are best suited for cyclists with very good fitness levels.

Q: Can I visit the Calar Alto Observatory by bike?
A: Absolutely! Reaching the Calar Alto Astronomical Observatory is a popular and rewarding challenge for touring cyclists. Routes like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1373684535/calar-alto-circular-desde-gergal" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Calar Alto — circular desde Gérgal&lt;/a&gt; or &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20118814" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Merendera Recreation Area – Calar Alto (2,168 m) loop from Gérgal&lt;/a&gt; offer significant climbs to this impressive destination, which is one of Europe's largest observatories.

Q: What natural attractions can I explore while touring cycling near Gérgal?
A: The region around Gérgal is rich in natural beauty. You can cycle through the unique landscapes of the Tabernas Desert, exploring areas like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/635124" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Tabernas Oasis&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3988407" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Tabernas Desert Natural Area&lt;/a&gt;. The Sierra de los Filabres offers stunning mountain scenery. You might also encounter impressive geological features such as &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4525156" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Barranco del Cautivo&lt;/a&gt; or &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6042540" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Rambla de los Yesos&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there any historical or cultural points of interest along the touring cycling routes?
A: Yes, Gérgal and its surroundings offer several historical and cultural points of interest. Gérgal itself features a castle, whitewashed houses, and a Mudejar church. In the Tabernas Desert, you can find unique attractions like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6419315" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Tabernas Western Village Andalusia&lt;/a&gt; and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4599892" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Texas Hollywood&lt;/a&gt;, which are old Western movie sets. Some routes, like the historical Water Route, also pass by old railway stations, traditional villages, and farmhouses.

Q: What do other touring cyclists say about the routes in Gérgal?
A: The touring cycling routes in Gérgal are highly regarded by the komoot community, holding an average rating of 4.2 stars from over 20 reviews. More than 500 touring cyclists have already used komoot to explore this region, often praising the diverse landscapes, challenging climbs, and unique desert scenery.

Q: Are there any circular touring cycling routes around Gérgal?
A: Yes, Gérgal offers several excellent circular touring cycling routes. A popular option is &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1373684535/calar-alto-circular-desde-gergal" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Calar Alto — circular desde Gérgal&lt;/a&gt;, which takes you on a significant climb and loop. Another great choice is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1373691092/gergal-olula-de-castro-and-tabernas-desert-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Gérgal, Olula de Castro and Tabernas Desert Loop&lt;/a&gt;, which allows you to experience both mountain and desert landscapes.

Q: Are there any easier touring cycling routes for beginners in Gérgal?
A: While Gérgal is known for its challenging routes, there are a few options suitable for beginners or those seeking a less strenuous ride. Out of the total routes, 2 are classified as easy and 6 as moderate. These routes typically feature gentler slopes and less demanding distances, allowing you to enjoy the scenery without extreme effort. Always check the route details on komoot for specific difficulty and elevation profiles.

Q: Where can I find parking for touring cycling routes in Gérgal?
A: As Gérgal is a small town, you can typically find street parking within the village itself, especially near the town center or residential areas. For specific routes starting outside the main village, it's advisable to check the route description on komoot for any suggested parking spots or to look for suitable roadside parking in nearby hamlets, ensuring you do not obstruct local access.

Q: Can I combine touring cycling with exploring the Tabernas Desert?
A: Absolutely! The Tabernas Desert is right on Gérgal's doorstep and offers a truly unique cycling experience. Routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1373691092/gergal-olula-de-castro-and-tabernas-desert-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Gérgal, Olula de Castro and Tabernas Desert Loop&lt;/a&gt; are specifically designed to take you through this arid landscape, allowing you to witness its distinctive badlands and
tips: Nice, small and relaxed place before/after the impressive desert.
Nice shady spots, benches and beautiful views.

The Calar Alto observatory, officially the Hispanic Astronomical Center in Andalusia (CAHA), is located on a plateau at 2,168 meters high in the Sierra de Filábres, in Almería.

Founded in 1973 after an agreement between the German and Spanish governments, it is the largest astronomical observatory in Europe. It has five telescopes, including the largest in continental Europe, with an aperture of 3.5 m.

Operated jointly by the Government of Andalusia and the Institute of Astrophysics of Andalusia - CSIC, it focuses its research on the search for exoplanets, star formation and galactic structures.

Sierra de Baza and Sierra de Los Filabres are part of a remote mountain range over 2.000m height in the province of Granada and Almería, and the most isolated area of Badlands.

This mountain range was a historic crossroads of cultures, and its legacy includes remains of Roman aqueducts or Arab castles.

At the highest point of the range lies the iconic Calar Alto Observatory (2.168m), easy recognizable from fara way.

The range also includes one of the most iconic and spectacular mountain pass in the Iberian Peninsula, el Puerto de Velefique (1.820m), popular by the high number of hairpin in the South side.

The South limit of Sierra de los Filabres reaches the village of Gérgal, considered the North gate of the Tabernas Desert.
